_meta.description: Risk register + incident response + business continuity + insurance. Drives /security-disclosure responses and incident-response runbook.

riskRegister.title: Founder medical incapacitation
riskRegister.description: Operator has hypoparathyroidism (E20.9) with critical Ca limits 8.0-9.0 mg/dL. Acute episode could remove sole executor for days to weeks.
mitigation: Recruit Vice-Chair with operational signing authority (governance.json successionPlanning)
Document all canonical processes in /docs/runbooks/ so any board member can execute
Treasurer + Secretary jointly authorized for 90-day continuity per bylaws
Operator maintains <$1000 daily-operations buffer accessible via dual-control
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: partial — runbooks exist, Vice-Chair seat vacant

riskRegister.title: Cloudflare free-tier policy change
riskRegister.description: Mission depends on free-tier Workers, Pages, KV, D1. Pricing or limit changes could force budget infrastructure spend.
mitigation: Stay under all free-tier limits (10ms CPU, 1000 subrequests, 100k req/day)
Architecture is portable to other edge providers (Deno Deploy, Vercel, Bun)
Project Galileo enrollment when 501(c)(3) determination received
Reserve fund target: 12 months of paid-tier equivalent ($2400/year estimate)

riskRegister.title: Charitable solicitation registration lapse in active state
riskRegister.description: Soliciting donations from residents of a state where unregistered exposes org to state AG action and donor refund obligations.
mitigation: Compliance.json tracks all 41 states requiring registration
Filing calendar generates annual reminders 60 days before each renewal
Initial deferral: register only in GA + states donating $1000+; expand from there
Geo-block donation flow from unregistered states until registered (ethical-degradation pattern)
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: deferred until donations begin; tracker ready

riskRegister.title: Donor data breach
riskRegister.description: Donor PII (name, address, payment method, gift history) compromise could trigger GDPR/state breach notification + donor trust loss.
mitigation: Stripe holds all payment-method data; we never store card numbers
Donor names + amounts stored in encrypted KV with access control
Quarterly review of who has access (principle of least privilege)
Cyber insurance once revenue threshold permits
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: good — minimal data stored; insurance pending

riskRegister.title: Mission drift toward enterprise SaaS
riskRegister.description: Pressure (from founders, donors, or board) to monetize mesh tools as B2B SaaS contradicts copyleft/edge-only ethos.
mitigation: Mission statement encoded in canon; programs.json values are publicly committed
Board adopts written 'mission lock' — material mission changes require supermajority + 60-day public-comment period
AGPL licensing makes enterprise extraction without contribution-back economically unattractive
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: structurally good — bylaws-flavored protection in canon

riskRegister.title: Single major donor dependency
riskRegister.description: If first major gift comes from one donor, premature dependency creates governance risk (donor exits = org collapse) and mission-capture risk.
mitigation: Target: no donor > 25% of annual revenue after year 2
Diversify funding sources (grants, individual, recurring, events)
Restricted-fund mechanics tracked in financials.json
Decline-policy enforces this when alignment is at risk (donor-policy.json ethicalScreening)
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: no donors yet — pre-empt by structuring this from day one

riskRegister.title: Burnout / operator spoon deficit
riskRegister.description: AuDHD + chronic illness + sole-executor status compounds. Crisis episodes deplete capacity for weeks.
mitigation: Cognitive-passport practice for self (the dogfood)
Spoon-deficit mode in tooling (terminal-only output, no chrome)
No artificial deadlines; canonical sources let work be paused without loss
Operator self-care is a fundable activity, not a luxury
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: structural mitigations strong; recovery still requires real time

riskRegister.title: IRS denial of 501(c)(3) determination
riskRegister.description: Application could be rejected for any reason; would invalidate retroactive deductibility of contributions made under 'pending' status.
mitigation: All Form 1023 narrative drawn from canon (programs.json, governance.json) — verifiable claims
Conservative interpretation of substantial-mission test
Donor-policy explicitly notes pending status; no false claim of current 501(c)(3)
If denied: convert to 501(c)(4) or for-profit B-corp; refund deductibility-conditioned gifts
riskRegister.currentMitigationStatus: application not yet filed; canon supports strong narrative

riskRegister.title: Hostile regulatory change (encryption ban, decentralized-tech restrictions)
riskRegister.description: Mission depends on regulatory protection of encryption + private-by-default tech.
mitigation: Mission could pivot to advocacy if technical layer is foreclosed
Diversify across jurisdictions when scale permits
Public-record canon ensures continuity of intent even if implementation must change

classes.criteria: Active data breach, payment system fraud, operator medical emergency
Service outage, security vuln with active exploit, regulatory deadline missed
Non-active vuln, contractual breach, donor complaint
Minor bug, doc inaccuracy, low-impact dispute

responseTeam.noteOnFlatStructure: All roles currently held by Operator; succession plan activates dual-control on incapacitation

scenarios.response: No action needed; canonical sources keep ship-bar passing
Treasurer + Secretary maintain operations; no policy changes; no funds movement >$1000; statement to public if revenue depends on visible activity
Vice-Chair (when seated) acts as Chair; full operational continuity; board considers 90-day plan if extended
Full board meeting within 30d to assess: continue, transfer mission to allied org, or wind down. Bylaws govern dissolution clause.

donorRecords.secondary: weekly export to encrypted local + offsite copy

neededPolicies.trigger: before first board member outside founder is seated
first in-person event OR first physical office
annual revenue > $50k OR storing donor PII > 1000 records

responseSLA.patchTimeline: varies by severity (P0: 24h, P1: 7d, P2: 30d, P3: next release)
